45-minute drive back from North Bend to Renton after the trail run.

beats.activity: Prepare and enjoy dinner at home, then start a watercolor painting.
beats.reasoning: Quinn unwinds with dinner and creative time; painting is a cherished leisure activity for the optimistic researcher.

diary: Monday felt like a gift—sunny and warm, the kind of day that reminds you why you live here. Started with coffee and the forecast (occupational habit), then drove into the city for work. The light rail was down, but the drive was smooth enough. I was supposed to be deep in climate models all morning, but honestly, I kept thinking about that USA-Wales match. Around 11, I gave in and walked over to Zeitgeist. Something about sitting with a cortado and watching the game in that café, surrounded by other people who'd abandoned their Monday plans—it felt good. We played well. After, I walked back to the office, tried to refocus on the impact assessments, sat through a team meeting. But my mind was already halfway to the mountains. By evening I was at Rattlesnake Lake, running the trails with the Cascades turning gold in the last light. My legs felt strong. Back home, I made dinner, and then I just... started painting. Nothing planned, just watercolors and the quiet hum of the house. Felt settled.
thought_process: I think the weather had more influence on me than I realized. Sunny days at this latitude in early June are rare enough that they shift something—they make you want to move, to be outside, to break routine. I almost skipped the café for the match, kept telling myself I should stay productive, but the moment I got there I knew it was the right call. Work felt less urgent when I came back; the models will still be there tomorrow. The evening run was the real reset though. I've been anxious about some of the climate data we're seeing, and I needed that physicality, that reminder that my body can still move through the world. The watercolor at the end was pure impulse—no strategy, just color and water. Some days you need to earn your rest.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
